page: Microsoft Azure F2 is an F-family virtual machine configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Local storage is limited to a bundled 33 GB volume, and there are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the F2 instance operates in the lower performance tiers. In stress-ng and Geekbench testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU metrics fall within the bottom 10% of recorded results. Memory bandwidth is similarly constrained, with read-only large block transfers registering at 14.41 GB/sec and write speeds at 10.79 GB/sec. Cryptographic and database operations, such as Redis SET commands, also place in the bottom 10%, though the processor's support for AVX, AES, and FMA instructions helps it achieve a slightly higher weak rating in Passmark extended instruction tests and small-model LLM inference.

From a resource density perspective, the F2 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation rather than shared-core burstable resources. However, the low memory-to-CPU ratio and poor overall benchmark performance present a clear tradeoff, making it unsuitable for memory-intensive applications or high-throughput databases. This instance is best suited for lightweight utility services, basic static web serving, minor text processing, or simple development and testing tasks that do not demand high computational or memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an F-family virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, a 33 GB local storage volume, and no GPU resources. Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the bottom 10% for CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though it shows slightly better results in extended instruction tests. Given its low memory-to-CPU ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, the F2 represents a budget-friendly option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic static web serving, simple text processing, and low-demand development or testing environments where high computational throughput and large memory capacities are not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an F-family virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an Intel-powered virtual machine with 2 dedicated vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual machine with dedicated dual-core processing and low memory allocation for basic utility workloads and development tasks.

Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and a 33 GB local storage volume
Delivers entry-level performance suitable for low-demand applications
